title: Expressing support for the designation of "Zoroastrian Legacy and Heritage Month".
plain_summary: This bill supports making March a special month to honor Zoroastrians and their heritage. Zoroastrianism is one of the world's oldest religions that has helped shape ideas about human rights and democracy. The bill wants to recognize important Zoroastrian leaders like Cyrus the Great and celebrate their holiday called Nowruz that happens in March. This would help people learn about and appreciate the contributions Zoroastrians have made to our world.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform.
policy_area: Civil Rights and Liberties, Minority Issues

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This resolution expresses support for naming March as Zoroastrian Legacy and Heritage Month.

sections.title: Reasons for Supporting Zoroastrian Heritage Month
sections.text: The House of Representatives recognizes that Zoroastrianism is one of the world's oldest religions that worships one god. It began in ancient Persia and has made important contributions to history and civilization. This religion has greatly influenced the development of freedom, democracy, trade, and business around the world. Cyrus the Great was a follower of Zoroastrianism and is mentioned twenty-three times in the Old Testament of the Bible. In five hundred thirty-nine years before the common era, he created the first declaration of human rights on something called the Cyrus Cylinder. This declaration emphasized freedom of religion, equality, cultural and civil rights, and the end of slavery. The last month of the original Zoroastrian calendar falls in March and is devoted to Mother Earth. This represents equality, devotion to nature, humility, love, peace, and harmony for all people. The spring festival of Nowruz, also called Novruz, happens on the day of the vernal equinox in March. This is the most important day in the Zoroastrian calendar and represents the victory of light, warmth, and life over darkness. Zoroastrians are required to preserve and protect the divine creations of earth, air, water, plants, cattle, and humans. The first seven days of spring in March are considered very special for nature in the Zoroastrian calendar, but the celebration traditionally continues until the thirteenth day after Nowruz. Zoroastrian legacy has also significantly helped humanity throughout history in business, international trade, art, and culture.

sections.title: Resolution
sections.text: Therefore, the House of Representatives supports the designation of Zoroastrian Legacy and Heritage Month.
